title: rubus tomentosus subsp. tomentosifrondosus
intro: Rubus tomentosus subsp. tomentosifrondosus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus tomentosus species, a type of raspberry native to the Himalayas. The plant's medicinal properties include its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues due to its anthelmintic and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus tomentosus subsp. tomentosifrondosus include its ability to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic, which can help to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its leaves and roots are also used in traditional medicine to make medicines and remedies.
